title: Elementary School: Teacher Early Childhood Special Education
summary: Early Childhood Special Education teacher serving students ages 3-5 with disabilities. The role provides instruction in academic, behavior, communication, independent living, social, and personal care skills, and supports students who may have significant cognitive, behavioral, communication, or physical disabilities.
required_education: Bachelor's degree in Education or a related field
required_credentials: Valid Texas Education Agency teaching certification appropriate to subject and grade level, Texas Content Competency Worksheet demonstration, or statement of eligibility
Valid Special Education Certification EC-12 or Special Education Specialist Certification EC-12
Applied Behavior Analysis training within one year of hire
preferred_credentials: De-escalation/restraint certification such as CPI, or ability to obtain district-provided certification once hired
preferred_experience: Experience working with students with disabilities
Experience with early childhood special education
Experience using adaptive or assistive technology
Experience supporting IEP, ARD, PLAAFP, behavior, and progress-monitoring processes
responsibilities: Provide instruction in academic, behavior, communication, independent living, social, and personal care skills.
Serve children ages 3-5 in an Early Childhood Special Education program.
Collaborate with parents, students, and school or community personnel to develop measurable IEP goals aligned with Texas Pre-Kindergarten Guidelines.
Plan and implement curriculum with accommodations, modifications, and adaptations based on student needs.
Document ongoing student progress and maintain records such as service logs, IEP target data, behavior data, and progress updates.
Administer and analyze district, informal, and criterion-referenced assessments.
Develop PLAAFP statements and annual goals or objectives based on assessment results.
Prepare and implement developmentally appropriate lesson plans based on IEPs, behavior intervention plans, and curriculum modifications.
Use adaptive and assistive technology documented in ARD plans or recommended by related service providers.
Execute behavior strategies from ARD documents, including reinforcements, consequences, and contingencies.
Use approved behavior intervention strategies, including physical restraint when a student is in danger of harming self or others.
Provide school health services under nurse supervision, such as G-tube feedings or medication administration.
Assist students with personal care services including feeding, cleaning, dressing, lifting, positioning, transitioning, toileting, and changing.
Conduct professional activities consistent with state and federal law.
Safeguard confidentiality for students with disabilities.
Prepare paperwork for and participate in ARD meetings.
Participate in required or assigned professional development.
skills: Knowledge of current instructional research and theory
physical_requirements: Occasional walking, standing, sitting, bending, stooping, kneeling, crouching, crawling, and lifting or carrying work-related items under 40 pounds.
May assist with lifting and positioning students.
May work outside up to 25% of the time.
May be exposed to bodily fluids and common childhood illnesses.
May work prolonged or irregular hours.
rubric_hints: IEP, ARD, PLAAFP, and progress-monitoring experience
ABA training or ability to complete it
Personal care and adaptive technology experience
demo_notes: Strong hard-gate demo option because it requires Texas educator certification and Special Education EC-12 certification.
Good checkpoint example for non-certification requirements such as CPI, ABA training timeline, personal-care duties, and assistive-technology experience.
